title: Interactively Generating Explanations for Transformer Language Models

structured.summary: The paper presents Proto-Trex, a novel architecture embedding prototypical explanation layers within transformer language models to provide inherently interpretable, case-based explanations. It further introduces an interactive learning framework (iProto-Trex) enabling users to manipulate prototypes and refine explanations with variable certainty, aiming to enhance trust and human-AI collaboration in NLP tasks.

strengths.point: Integration of prototype-based explanations into transformer LMs
strengths.evidence: Proto-Trex embeds an interpretable prototype layer inside transformer models to provide case-based explanations referencing prototypical training examples.

strengths.point: Introduction of interactive prototype learning framework
strengths.evidence: iProto-Trex allows users to replace or steer prototypes with variable certainty, incorporating user knowledge directly into explanation refinement.
Experiments on Yelp and Movie review sentiment datasets show Proto-Trex variants perform on par with their respective baseline language models.

strengths.point: Supports human-AI collaboration and explanation steering
strengths.evidence: User interactions improve prototype alignment with preferences without accuracy degradation, demonstrating effective interactive explanation manipulation.

weaknesses.point: Limited novelty positioning against related prototype-based NLP methods
weaknesses.evidence: No explicit discussion or empirical comparison to previous prototype explainability approaches like ProtoPNet adaptations for text.

weaknesses.point: Insufficient methodological details and ablation studies
weaknesses.evidence: Lacks ablations on prototype layer size and placement, and omits justification or heuristics for prototype replacement strategies in interactive learning.

weaknesses.point: Evaluation focuses mainly on accuracy, with limited explanation quality assessment
weaknesses.evidence: No quantitative metrics such as fidelity or human-interpretability scores, nor user studies or statistical significance analyses on interactive learning results.

weaknesses.point: Restricted evaluation scope and scalability concerns
weaknesses.evidence: Experiments are limited to sentiment classification datasets; scalability and potential overfitting to prototypes are not discussed.

Incomplete technical details on prototype representation, similarity computations, interaction protocols, and missing training hyperparameters; code repository lacks documentation on interactive usage.

questions.question: How does Proto-Trex compare quantitatively and qualitatively to other prototype-based NLP interpretability methods?
questions.motivation: Understanding relative advantages or limitations is crucial for assessing novelty and practical impact.

questions.question: What are the specific heuristics or algorithms governing prototype replacement with varying certainty in the interactive learning framework?
questions.motivation: Clarifying this is important for reproducibility and evaluating the effectiveness of user interventions.

questions.question: Can the approach generalize to more complex NLP tasks beyond sentiment classification and scale efficiently to larger models or datasets?
questions.motivation: Assessing generalisability and scalability determines real-world applicability.

questions.question: How do the prototype-based explanations compare to transformer attention-based explanations in terms of interpretability and user trust?
questions.motivation: Since transformers already use attention mechanisms for interpretability, contrasting these approaches can clarify the added value.

critique_points.point_001: Limited novelty positioning against related prototype-based NLP methods. No explicit discussion or empirical comparison to previous prototype explainability approaches like ProtoPNet adaptations for text
critique_points.point_002: Insufficient methodological details and ablation studies. Lacks ablations on prototype layer size and placement, and omits justification or heuristics for prototype replacement strategies in interactive learning
critique_points.point_003: Evaluation focuses mainly on accuracy, with limited explanation quality assessment. No quantitative metrics such as fidelity or human-interpretability scores, nor user studies or statistical significance analyses on interactive learning results
critique_points.point_004: Restricted evaluation scope and scalability concerns. Experiments are limited to sentiment classification datasets; scalability and potential overfitting to prototypes are not discussed
critique_points.point_005: Clarity and reproducibility issues. Incomplete technical details on prototype representation, similarity computations, interaction protocols, and missing training hyperparameters; code repository lacks documentation on interactive usage
